benchmark_metadata.methodology: Scientific measurement with statistical analysis and brutal honesty

savage_analysis.roasting_summary: 60% of commands violate simplicity rules with complexity scores ≥ 4.5. That's not 'intelligent design', that's complexity addiction.

adhd-morning-assistant.roast: 308 lines to say 'make a todo list'? Your ADHD assistant has ADHD. The irony is thicc.
adhd-morning-assistant.evidence: 7 MCP integrations for morning planning. NASA uses less complexity to plan Mars missions.
adhd-morning-assistant.improvement: Remove 80% of the nested thinking blocks. ADHD brains need SIMPLE, not 'ultra-meta-cognitive-planning-orchestration'.

adaptive-complexity-router.roast: A 498-line command to decide how to think? That's like building a factory to decide which hammer to use.
adaptive-complexity-router.evidence: 22 thinking blocks to route thinking. The irony meter just exploded and set my office on fire.
adaptive-complexity-router.improvement: Replace with: if (simple) { doIt(); } else { thinkThenDoIt(); }. You're welcome.

java-clean-code-generator.roast: 818 lines preaching simplicity while being the most complex command. This is peak programmer hypocrisy.
java-clean-code-generator.evidence: 28 thinking blocks about 'simplicity first' in a 4653-token monstrosity. Chef's kiss of irony.
java-clean-code-generator.improvement: Practice what you preach. Start by deleting 600 lines of your own complexity.

ultrathink.roast: 356 lines to think deeply. Socrates managed with fewer words and no XML blocks.
ultrathink.evidence: 11 layers of meta-thinking about thinking. That's not deep, that's a cognitive sinkhole.
ultrathink.improvement: Replace with simple prompt: 'Think step by step'. Works 90% as well with 99% less pretension.

senior-developer-analysis.roast: Most verbose per line at 4.04 ratio. Senior developers explain simply. This explains verbosely.
senior-developer-analysis.evidence: 18 thinking blocks to explain code. Real seniors just read the damn code.
senior-developer-analysis.improvement: Cut the philosophical waffle. Show code, explain purpose, done.

immediate_actions: Delete 60% of all thinking blocks - they're mental masturbation
Remove complexity routing - just do the damn task
Consolidate overlapping commands - you have 5 ways to think about thinking
Apply your own simplicity rules to your commands
systematic_improvements: Implement complexity budget: 200 lines max per command
Ban nested thinking blocks deeper than 2 levels
Require justification for every XML block
Add 'stupid simple' variants of complex commands
philosophical_guidance: Commands should DO things, not philosophize about doing things
If you need a PhD to understand your command, you've failed
Complexity is not intelligence - it's the opposite
The best command is the one that doesn't exist because you used existing tools

benchmark_conclusion.final_wisdom: You've created an AI that needs an AI to understand its own commands. The singularity isn't coming - it's here, and it's writing 800-line functions to say 'hello world'.
